author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

title: John 16.16,17

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “A little while, and ye shall not see Me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see Me, because I go to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then said some of His disciples among themselves, What is this that He saith?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: [And what follows.]

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nothing is wont so to cast down the soul that is anguished and possessed by deep despondency, as when words which cause pain are continually dwelt upon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then did Christ, after saying, “I go,” and, “Hereafter I will not speak with you,” continually dwell on the same subject, saying, “A little while, and ye shall not see Me, because I go to Him that sent Me”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He had recovered them by His words concerning the Spirit, He again casteth down their courage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore doth He this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He testeth their feelings, and rendereth them more proved, and well accustometh them by hearing sad things, manfully to bear separation from Him; for they who had practiced this when spoken of in words, were likely in actions also, easily to bear it afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if one enquire closely, this very thing is a consolation, the saying that, “I go to the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is the expression of One, who declares that He shall not perish, but that His end is a kind of translation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He addeth too another consolation; for He saith not merely, “A little while, and ye shall not see Me,” but also, “A little while, and ye shall see Me”; showing that He will both come to them again, and that their separation would be but for a little while, and His presence with them continual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This, however, they did not understand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence one may with reason wonder how, after having often heard these things, they doubt, as though they had heard nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then is it that they did not understand?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was either through grief, as I suppose, for that drove what was said from their understanding; or through the obscurity of the words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He seemed to them to set forth two contraries, which were not contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If,” saith one of them, “we shall see Thee, whither goest Thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if Thou goest, how shall we see Thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore they say, “We cannot tell what He saith.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That He was about to depart, they knew; but they knew not that He would shortly come to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account He rebuketh them, because they did not understand His saying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, desiring to infix in them the doctrine concerning His death, what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament”—which belonged to the Death and the Cross—“but the world shall rejoice.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because by reason of their not desiring His death, they quickly ran into the belief that He would not die, and then when they heard that He would die, cast about, not knowing what that “little” meant, He saith, “Ye shall mourn and lament.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But your sorrow shall be turned into joy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then having shown that after grief comes joy, and that grief gendereth joy, and that grief is short, but the pleasure endless, He passeth to a common example; and what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He hath used a comparison which the Prophets also use continually, likening despondencies to the exceeding pains of childbirth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what He saith is of this kind: “Travail pains shall lay hold on you, but the pang of childbirth is the cause of joy”; both confirming His words relative to the Resurrection, and showing that the departing hence is like passing from the womb into the light of day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “Marvel not that I bring you to your advantage through such sorrow, since even a mother to become a mother, passeth in like manner through pain.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here also He implieth something mystical, that He hath loosened the travail pangs of death, and caused a new man to be born of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He said not, that the pain shall pass away only, but, “she doth not even remember it,” so great is the joy which succeedeth; so also shall it be with the Saints.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet the woman doth not rejoice because “a man hath come into the world,” but because a son hath been born to her; since, had this been the case, nothing would have hindered the barren from rejoicing over another who beareth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then spake He thus?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He introduced this example for this purpose only, to show that sorrow is for a season, but joy lasting: and to show that (death) is a translation unto life; and to show the great profit of their pangs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “a child hath been born,” but, “A man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to my mind He here alludeth to His own Resurrection, and that He should be born not unto that death which bare the birth-pang, but unto the Kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He said not, “a child hath been born unto her,” but, “A man hath been born into the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And ye now therefore have sorrow—[but I will see you again, and your sorrow shall be turned into joy].”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, to show that He shall die no more, He saith, “And no man taketh it from you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in that day ye shall ask Me nothing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again He proveth nothing else by these words, but that He is from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For then ye shall for the time to come know all things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is, “Ye shall not ask Me”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye shall need no intercessor, but it is sufficient that ye call on My Name, and so gain all things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall ask My Father in My Name.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth the power of His Name, if at least being neither seen nor called upon, but only named, He even maketh us approved by the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But where hath this taken place?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Where they say, “Lord, behold their threatenings, and grant unto Thy servants that with boldness they may speak Thy word” ( Acts iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29, 31 ), “and work miracles in Thy Name.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And the place was shaken where they were.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Hitherto ye have asked nothing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hence He showeth it to be good that He should depart, if hitherto they had asked nothing, and if then they should receive all things whatsoever they should ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For do not suppose, because I shall no longer be with you, that ye are deserted; My Name shall give you greater boldness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then the words which He had used had been veiled, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs, but the time cometh when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “There shall be a time when ye shall know all things clearly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He speaketh of the time of the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I shall tell you plainly of the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: (For He was with them, and talked with them forty days, being assembled with them, and speaking of the things concerning the kingdom of God— Acts i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3, 4 ,)—“because now being in fear, ye give no heed to My words; but then when ye see Me risen again, and converse with Me, ye will be able to learn all things plainly, for the Father Himself will love you, when your faith in Me hath been made firm.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I will not ask the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Your love for Me sufficeth to be your advocate.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because ye have loved Me, and have believed that I came out from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I came forth from the Father, and am come into the world; again I leave the world, and go to the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since His discourse concerning the Resurrection, and together with this, the hearing that “I came out from God, and thither I go,” gave them no common comfort, He continually handleth these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He gave a pledge, in the first place, that they were right in believing on Him; in the second, that they should be in safety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore He said, “A little while, and ye shall not see Me; and again a little while, and ye shall see Me” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 ), they with reason did not understand Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now it is no longer so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is, “Ye shall not ask Me”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye shall not say, ‘Show us the Father,’ and, ‘Whither goest Thou?’ for ye shall know all knowledge, and the Father shall be disposed towards you even as I am.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was this especially which made them breathe again, the learning that they should be the Father’s friends wherefore they say,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now we know that Thou knowest all things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He made answer to what was secretly harboring in their minds?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And needest not that any man should ask Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “Before hearing, Thou knowest the things which made us stumble, and Thou hast given us rest, since Thou hast said, ‘The Father loveth you, because ye have loved Me.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After so many and so great matters, they say, “Now we know.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou in what an imperfect state they were?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, when, as though conferring a favor upon Him, they say, “Now we know,” He replieth, “Ye still require many other things to come to perfection; nothing is as yet achieved by you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye shall presently betray Me to My enemies, and such fear shall seize you, that ye shall not even be able to retire one with another, yet from this I shall suffer nothing dreadful.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou again how con descending His speech is?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And indeed He makes this a charge against them, that they continually needed condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when they say, “Lo, now Thou speakest plainly, and speakest no parable” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29 ), “and therefore we believe Thee,” He showeth them that now, when they believe, they do not yet believe, neither doth He accept their words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He saith, referring them to another season.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Father is with Me,” He hath again put on their account; for this they everywhere wished to learn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, to show that He did not give them perfect knowledge by saying this, but in order that their reason might not rebel, (for it was probable that they might form some human ideas, and think that they should not enjoy any assistance from Him,) He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things I have spoken unto you, that in Me ye might have peace.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “that ye should not cast Me from your thoughts, but receive Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let no one, then, drag these words into a doctrine; they are spoken for our comfort and love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For not even when we suffer such things as I have mentioned shall your troubles stop there, but as long as ye are in the world ye shall have sorrow, not only now when I am betrayed, but also afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But rouse your minds, for ye shall suffer nothing terrible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When the master hath gotten the better of his enemies, the disciples must not despond.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And how,” tell me, “hast Thou ‘conquered the world’?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I have told you already, that I have cast down its ruler, but ye shall know hereafter, when all things yield and give place to you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But it is permitted to us also to conquer, looking to the Author of our faith, and walking on that road which He cut for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So neither shall death get the mastery of us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then, shall we not die?” saith some one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why, from this very thing it is clear that he shall not gain the mastery over us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The champion truly will then be glorious, not when he hath not closed with his opponent, but when having closed he is not holden by him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We therefore are not mortal, because of our struggle with death, but immortal, because of our victory; then should we have been mortal, had we remained with him always.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then I should not call the longest-lived animals immortal, although they long remain free from death, so neither him who shall rise after death mortal, because he is dissolved by death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, tell me, if a man blush a little, should we say that he was continually ruddy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not so, for the action is not a habit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If one become pale, should we call him jaundiced?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No, for the affection is but temporary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so you would not call him mortal, who hath been for but a short time in the hands of death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since in this way we may speak of those who sleep, for they are dead, so to say, and without action.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But doth death corrupt our bodies?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What of that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is not that they may remain in corruption, but that they be made better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then conquer the world, let us run to immortality, let us follow our King, let us too set up a trophy, let us despise the world’s pleasures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We need no toil to do so; let us transfer our souls to heaven, and all the world is conquered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou desirest it not, it is conquered; if thou deride it, it is worsted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Strangers are we and sojourners, let us then not grieve at any of its painful things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if, being sprung from a renowned country, and from illustrious ancestors, thou hadst gone into some distant land, being known to no one, having with thee neither servants nor wealth, and then some one had insulted thee, thou wouldest not grieve as though thou hadst suffered these things at home.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the knowing clearly that thou wast in a strange and foreign land, would persuade thee to bear all easily, and to despise hunger, and thirst, and any suffering whatever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider this also now, that thou art a stranger and a sojourner, and let nothing disturb thee in this foreign land; for thou hast a City whose Artificer and Creator is God, and the sojourning itself is but for a short and little time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let whoever will strike, insult, revile; we are in a strange land, and live but meanly; the dreadful thing would be, to suffer so in our own country, before our fellow-citizens, then is the greatest unseemliness and loss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if a man be where he had none that knows him, he endures all easily, because insult becomes more grievous from the intention of those who offer it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For instance, if a man insult the governor, knowing that he is governor, then the insult is bitter; but if he insult, supposing him to be a private man, he cannot even touch him who undergoeth the insult.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So let us reason also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For neither do our revilers know what we are, as, that we are citizens of heaven, registered for the country which is above, fellow-choristers of the Cherubim.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then grieve nor deem their insult to be insult; had they known, they would not have insulted us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do they deem us poor and mean?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Neither let us count this an insult.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For tell me, if a traveler having got before his servants, were sitting a little space in the inn waiting for them, and then the innkeeper, or some travelers, should behave rudely to him, and revile him, would he not laugh at the other’s ignorance? would not their mistake rather give him pleasure? would he not feel a satisfaction as though not he but some one else were insulted?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us too behave thus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We too sit in an inn, waiting for our friends who travel the same road; when we are all collected, then they shall know whom they insult.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These men then shall hang their heads; then they shall say, “This is he whom we” fools “had in derision.” ( Wisd. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With these two things then let us comfort ourselves, that we are not insulted, for they know not who we are, and that, if we wish to obtain satisfaction, they shall hereafter give us a most bitter one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But God forbid that any should have a soul so cruel and inhuman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then if we be insulted by our kinsmen?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the burdensome thing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nay, this is the light thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why, pray?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because we do not bear those whom we love when they insult us, in the same way as we bear those whom we do not know.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For instance, in consoling those who have been injured, we often say, “It is a brother who hath injured you, bear it nobly; it is a father; it is an uncle.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if the name of “father” and “brother” puts you to shame, much more if I name to you a relationship more intimate than these; for we are not only brethren one to another, but also members, and one body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if the name of brother shame you, much more that of member.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard that Gentile proverb, which saith, that “it behooveth to keep friends with their defects”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard Paul say, “Bear ye one another’s burdens”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou not lovers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I am compelled, since I cannot draw an instance from you, to bring my discourse to that ground of argument.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also Paul doth, thus saying, “Furthermore we have had fathers in our flesh, which corrected us, and we gave them reverence.” ( Heb. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather, that is more apt which he saith to the Romans, “As ye have yielded your members servants to uncleanness and to iniquity unto iniquity, even so now yield your members servants to righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this reason let us confidently keep hold of the illustration.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now dost thou not observe lovers, what miseries these suffer when inflamed with desire for harlots, cuffed, beaten, and laughed at, enduring a harlot, who turns away from and insults them in ten thousand ways; yet if they see but once anything sweet or gentle, all is well to do with them, all former things are gone, all goes on with a fair wind, be it poverty, be it sickness, be it anything else besides these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they count their own life as miserable or blessed, according as they may have her whom they love disposed towards them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They know nothing of mortal honor or disgrace, but even if one insult, they bear all easily through the great pleasure and delight which they receive from her; and though she revile, though she spit in their face, they think, when they are enduring this, that they are being pelted with roses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what wonder, if such are their feelings as to her person? for her very house they think to be more splendid than any, though it be but of mud, though it be falling down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why speak I of walls? when they even see the places which they frequent in the evening, they are excited.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Allow me now for what follows to speak the word of the Apostle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As he saith, “As ye have yielded your members servants to uncleanness, so yield your members servants unto righteousness”; so in like manner now I say, “as we have loved these women, let us love one another, and we shall not think that we suffer anything terrible.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why say I, “one another”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us so love God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do ye shudder, when ye hear that I require as much love in the case of God, as we have shown towards a harlot?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But I shudder that we do not show even thus much.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, if you will, let us go on with the argument, though what is said be very painful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The woman beloved promises her lovers nothing good, but dishonor, shame, and insolence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is what the waiting upon a harlot makes a man, ridiculous, shameful, dishonored.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But God promiseth us heaven, and the good things which are in heaven; He hath made us sons, and brethren of the Only-begotten, and hath given thee ten thousand things while living, and when thou diest, resurrection, and promiseth that He will give us such good things as it is not possible even to imagine, and maketh us honored and revered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, that woman compels her lovers to spend all their substance for the pit and for destruction; but God biddeth us sow the heaven, and giveth us an hundred-fold, and eternal life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, she uses her lover like a slave, giving commands more hardly than any tyrant; but God saith, “I no longer call you servants, but friends.” ( c. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Have ye seen the excess both of the evils here and the blessings there ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then comes next?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this woman’s sake, many lie awake, and whatever she commands, readily obey; give up house, and father, and mother, and friends, and money, and patronage, and leave all that belongs to them in want and desolation; but for the sake of God, or rather for the sake of ourselves, we often do not choose to expend even the third portion of our substance, but we look on the hungry, we overlook him, and run past the naked, and do not even bestow a word upon him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the lovers, if they see but a little servant girl of their mistress, and her a barbarian, they stand in the middle of the market-place, and talk with her, as if they were proud and glad to do so, unrolling an interminable round of words; and for her sake they count all their living as nothing, deem rulers and rule nothing, (they know it, all who have had experience of the malady,) and thank her more when she commands, than others when they serve.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is there not with good reason a hell?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Are there not with good reason ten thousand punishments?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then become sober, let us apply to the service of God as much, or half, or even the third part of what others supply to the harlot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps again ye shudder; for so do I myself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But I would not that ye should shudder at words only, but at the actions; as it is, here indeed our hearts are made orderly, but we go forth and cast all away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is the gain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For there, if it be required to spend money, no one laments his poverty, but even borrows it to give, perchance, when smitten.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here, if we do but mention almsgiving, they pretend to us children, and wife, and house, and patronage, and ten thousand excuses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “the pleasure is great there.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This it is that I lament and mourn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What if I show that the pleasure here is greater?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For there shame, and insult, and expense, cut away no little of the pleasure, and after these the quarreling and enmity; but here there is nothing of the kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is there, tell me, equal to this pleasure, to sit expecting heaven and the kingdom there, and the glory of the saints, and the life that is endless?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But these things,” saith some one, “are in expectation, the others in experience.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What kind of experience?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wilt thou that I tell thee the pleasures which are here also by experience?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider what freedom thou enjoyest, and how thou fearest and tremblest at no man when thou livest in company with virtue, neither enemy, nor plotter, nor informer, nor rival in credit or in love, nor envious person, nor poverty, nor sickness, nor any other human thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But there, although ten thousand things be according to thy mind, though riches flow in as from a fountain, yet the war with rivals, and the plots, and ambuscades, will make more miserable than any the life of him who wallows with those women.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when that abominable one is haughty, and insolent, you needs must kindle quarrel to flatter her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This therefore is more grievous than ten thousand deaths, more intolerable than any punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here there is nothing of the kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “the fruit,” it saith, “of the Spirit is love, joy, peace.” ( Gal. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here is no quarreling, nor unseasonable pecuniary expense, nor disgrace and expense too; and if thou give but a farthing, or a loaf, or a cup of cold water, He will be much beholden to thee, and He doth nothing to pain or grieve thee, but all so as to make thee glorious, and free thee from all shame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What defense therefore shall we have, what pardon shall we gain, if, leaving these things, we give ourselves up to the contrary, and voluntarily cast ourselves into the furnace that burns with fire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore I exhort those who are sick of this malady, to recover themselves, and return to health, and not allow themselves to fall into despair.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since that son also was in a far more grievous state than this, yet when he returned to his father’s house, he came to his former honor, and appeared more glorious than him who had ever been well-pleasing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us also imitate him, and returning to our Father, even though it be late, let us depart from that captivity, and transfer ourselves to freedom, that we may enjoy the Kingdom of heaven, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom with the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, for ever and ever.
